_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to interpret and apply laws, regulations, policies, and guidance relevant to organization cyber objectives.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to translate complex regulatory requirements into clear, actionable organizational policies and operational procedures that non-legal staff can implement and verify.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-S-002 (RC, cosine: 0.618), RC-T-008 (RC, cosine: 0.597).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.705). Shared vocabulary: [ability, policies]. DCWF-only terms [apply, cyber, guidance, interpret, laws, objectives] vs WIDAI-only terms [actionable, clear, complex, implement, legal, non] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.884 (P=0.897, R=0.871). P exceeds R by 0.026 — the DCWF element's 15 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to interpret and apply laws, regulations, policies, and guidance relevan" and "Ability to translate complex regulatory requirements into clear, actionable orga" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.619 < STS 0.705 (gap -0.086). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [apply, cyber, guidance] and [actionable, clear, complex] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.3%. Shared tokens: [ability, policies]. DCWF-only: [apply, cyber, guidance, interpret, laws, objectives]. WIDAI-only: [actionable, clear, complex, implement, legal, non].
